description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a predominantly off-white coloration and several brownish stains and patches. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring sharp edges, angular facets, and visible chipping, suggesting it may have been shaped or fractured by human or natural forces. There are handwritten markings in black ink on one of the broader faces, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The stone's texture is coarse, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to wear or handling. Its overall form is asymmetrical, with one side broader and flatter, while the opposite edge tapers to a thinner, more pointed profile. The stone appears dense and solid, typical of flint or chert commonly found in urban landscaping or archaeological contexts.
description: An irregular, off-white stone with brown stains, rough texture, and handwritten markings on one face. Its angular, chipped form suggests natural or intentional shaping.
description_view_0: The broadest face of the stone is visible, showing handwritten markings and several brown stains on a rough, off-white surface.
description_view_1: A narrow, edge-on view reveals the stone's thickness and highlights its angular, pointed profile with visible brown discoloration.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, with fewer markings but clear evidence of chipping and a mix of off-white and brown coloration.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, displaying the stone's irregular thickness and the transition between rough and slightly smoother surfaces.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with brown stains and handwritten markings; no natural veining or regular patterning.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough with some smoother worn areas and visible chipping, indicating moderate weathering and handling.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; not embedded or covered by vegetation.
